# Notifications List Component (React Machine Coding Interview)

A **Notifications List** is a common frontend interview question that tests:

* List rendering
* Read/Unread state
* Infinite scroll / Pagination
* Real-time updates
* Performance optimisation
* Component design

For large notification feeds, consider pagination or list virtualization to avoid rendering thousands of DOM nodes. Libraries such as `react-virtualized` and `react-virtuoso` are commonly used for efficient rendering of large lists. [\[github.com\]](https://github.com/bvaughn/react-virtualized), [\[github.com\]](https://github.com/petyosi/react-virtuoso), [\[blog.logrocket.com\]](https://blog.logrocket.com/rendering-large-lists-react-virtualized/)

***

# Requirements

```text
✅ Show notifications

✅ Read / Unread state

✅ Mark as read

✅ Delete notification

✅ Timestamp

✅ Infinite scroll

✅ Real-time updates
```

***

# Data Model

```ts
interface Notification {
  id: string;

  title: string;

  message: string;

  type:
    | "info"
    | "warning"
    | "success"
    | "error";

  isRead: boolean;

  createdAt: string;
}
```

***

# Sample Data

```js
const notifications = [
  {
    id: "1",
    title: "Payment Success",
    message:
      "Your payment was successful",

    isRead: false,

    type: "success",

    createdAt:
      "2026-07-02T08:00:00Z",
  },

  {
    id: "2",
    title: "Profile Updated",

    message:
      "Profile updated successfully",

    isRead: true,

    type: "info",

    createdAt:
      "2026-07-02T09:00:00Z",
  },
];
```

***

# Component Structure

```text
NotificationsPage
        │
        ▼
 NotificationsList
        │
 ┌──────┼──────┐
 ▼      ▼      ▼
Header Item Footer
```

***

# Notification Item

```tsx
function NotificationItem({
  notification,
  onRead,
}) {
  return (
    <div
      className={`item ${
        notification.isRead
          ? ""
          : "unread"
      }`}
    >
      <h4>
        {
          notification.title
        }
      </h4>

      <p>
        {
          notification.message
        }
      </p>

      <small>
        {
          notification.createdAt
        }
      </small>

      {!notification.isRead && (
        <button
          onClick={() =>
            onRead(
              notification.id
            )
          }
        >
          Mark Read
        </button>
      )}
    </div>
  );
}
```

***

# Notifications List

```tsx
function NotificationsList() {
  const [
    notifications,
    setNotifications,
  ] = useState([]);

  const markAsRead = id => {
    setNotifications(prev =>
      prev.map(
        notification =>
          notification.id === id
            ? {
                ...notification,
                isRead: true,
              }
            : notification
      )
    );
  };

  return (
    <div>
      {notifications.map(
        notification => (
          <NotificationItem
            key={
              notification.id
            }
            notification={
              notification
            }
            onRead={
              markAsRead
            }
          />
        )
      )}
    </div>
  );
}
```

***

# Unread Count

```tsx
const unreadCount =
  notifications.filter(
    notification =>
      !notification.isRead
  ).length;
```

```tsx
<h2>
  Notifications (
  {unreadCount})
</h2>
```

***

# Delete Notification

```js
const deleteNotification =
  id => {
    setNotifications(prev =>
      prev.filter(
        notification =>
          notification.id !== id
      )
    );
  };
```

***

# Mark All Read

```js
const markAllRead = () => {
  setNotifications(prev =>
    prev.map(
      notification => ({
        ...notification,
        isRead: true,
      })
    )
  );
};
```

***

# Real-Time Updates (WebSocket)

```js
socket.on(
  "notification",
  newNotification => {
    setNotifications(
      prev => [
        newNotification,
        ...prev,
      ]
    );
  }
);
```

***

# Infinite Scroll

```tsx
useEffect(() => {
  const loadMore =
    async () => {
      const nextPage =
        await fetchNotifications(
          page
        );

      setNotifications(
        prev => [
          ...prev,
          ...nextPage,
        ]
      );
    };

  window.addEventListener(
    "scroll",
    loadMore
  );

  return () =>
    window.removeEventListener(
      "scroll",
      loadMore
    );
}, []);
```

For high-volume notification feeds, infinite scrolling may still render a large number of elements. Virtualisation can limit rendering to visible items and improve performance. [\[blog.logrocket.com\]](https://blog.logrocket.com/rendering-large-lists-react-virtualized/), [\[medium.com\]](https://medium.com/@syketweb/building-a-smooth-virtualized-list-in-react-0864120fb598)

***

# Notifications Dropdown

```text
🔔 5

----------------
Payment Success
Profile Updated
Order Delivered
----------------

View All
```

```tsx
<Badge count={unreadCount}>
  <BellIcon />
</Badge>
```

***

# Production Optimisations

### Pagination

```text
Page 1
Page 2
Page 3
```

### Virtualisation

```text
10,000 notifications
```

Use:

```text
react-window
react-virtualized
react-virtuoso
```

Virtualisation renders only visible items, dramatically reducing memory and rendering overhead for large lists. [\[github.com\]](https://github.com/bvaughn/react-virtualized), [\[github.com\]](https://github.com/petyosi/react-virtuoso), [\[blog.logrocket.com\]](https://blog.logrocket.com/rendering-large-lists-react-virtualized/)

***

# Accessibility

&lt;ul role="list"&gt;![Visualization](data:image/png;base64,)

&lt;li role="listitem"&gt;![Visualization](data:image/png;base64,)

aria-live="polite"![Visualization](data:image/png;base64,)

For real-time notifications.

***

# Senior Interview Answer

> I would build the notifications list as a reusable component with separate NotificationItem and NotificationList layers. State would store notifications and unread counts, while actions would support mark-as-read, delete, and mark-all-read. For production use, I would add WebSocket-based real-time updates, pagination or virtualisation for large feeds, and accessibility support with proper ARIA roles.
